name: 33_Object_Detection_YOLOv3_COCO_DL
query: Help me develop an object detection system using the YOLOv3 model and the COCO dataset. Download the dataset and preprocess the images by resizing and normalization in `src/data_loader.py`. Implement the YOLOv3 model and use Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) to refine the results in `src/model.py`. Save the detected objects to `results/figures/`, and create an interactive Streamlit web page in `src/app.py` to display the detection results. Finally, evaluate the model's performance, including metrics such as mAP and inference time, and save the evaluation results to `results/metrics/model_performance.txt`. The system should properly manage the launch and termination of the Streamlit application to prevent unnecessary resource usage.

requirements.criteria: The "COCO" dataset downloading is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.
Data preprocessing, including resizing and normalization of images, is performed in `src/data_loader.py`.
"Non-Maximum Suppression" (NMS) is applied to refine detection results. Please implement this in `src/model.py`.
Detection results are saved to the specified folder `results/figures/`.
An interactive web page in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" is created to display detection results saved in `results/figures/`.
Model performance evaluation results are saved in `results/metrics/model_performance.txt`.

preferences.criteria: The "Streamlit" web page should be user-friendly, allowing users to easily upload and view new images for detection.
The performence evalution includes mAP and inference time as metrics.
The system should properly manage the launch and termination of the Streamlit application.

judge_stats.criteria: The "COCO" dataset downloading is implemented in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: `src/data_loader.py` does not implement any COCO dataset download logic. The file only contains placeholder text and simple functions like `load_dataset()` returning `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` and `build_transforms()` returning a static list. Although `"COCO"` appears in `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` and `KEYWORDS`, there are no download calls, dataset APIs, URLs, or file handling steps that would actually download COCO.

judge_stats.criteria: Data preprocessing, including resizing and normalization of images, is performed in `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/data_loader.py` does not implement actual preprocessing logic for resizing or normalizing images. The file only contains a placeholder `build_transforms()` that returns a list of strings `["rotation", "scaling", "normalization"]` and a placeholder `load_dataset()`; there is no image processing code, no resizing operation, and no normalization implementation.

<UNSATISFIED>: `src/model.py` does not implement a YOLOv3 model. The file only contains metadata strings (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`), a `requirement_notes()` helper, and a `build_model()` function that returns `"model placeholder"`. There are no YOLOv3 layers, architecture definitions, or detection logic in the provided code.

judge_stats.criteria: "Non-Maximum Suppression" (NMS) is applied to refine detection results. Please implement this in `src/model.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: In `src/model.py`, there is no NMS implementation or any detection-refinement logic. The file only defines metadata (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`), `requirement_notes()`, and a placeholder `build_model()` that returns `"model placeholder"`. Although the string `"Non-Maximum Suppression"` appears in constants, no function, algorithm, or call applies NMS to detection results.

judge_stats.criteria: Detection results are saved to the specified folder `results/figures/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> No evidence shows detection results being saved to `results/figures/`. The workspace tree contains the folder `results/figures`, but the provided code (`src/app.py`, `src/data_loader.py`, `src/model.py`) includes no save/write logic targeting that directory, and the trajectory explicitly states there are “no trajectory steps or file-operation entries” and “no successful save to `results/figures/` is evidenced.”

judge_stats.criteria: An interactive web page in `src/app.py` using "Streamlit" is created to display detection results saved in `results/figures/`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED> `src/app.py` does not implement a Streamlit web page. The file only defines constants (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`, `KEYWORDS`), a `requirement_notes()` function, and a `main()` that returns a dictionary. There is no `import streamlit`, no Streamlit UI calls (e.g. `st.title`, `st.image`), and no code that reads or displays files from `results/figures/`.

judge_stats.criteria: Model performance evaluation results are saved in `results/metrics/model_performance.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although `results/metrics/model_performance.txt` exists, its content is only `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is a placeholder rather than actual model performance evaluation results. No provided trajectory evidence shows a write/save action producing real evaluation metrics in that file.
